description: When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for DHCP Relay?
Feature Caveat: DHCP Relay - When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: Switch is unable to connect to Central. Scenario: This issue may occur when attempting to onboard switches that are connected to Central. The process connecting to YANG is creating more sockets than expected, leading to overload of the yang-resolverd server and subsequent disconnections. Workaround: Restart yang-resolverd daemon to reestablish the operation of the daemon. Restart hpe-restd daemon to make a clean connection to YANG after restarting the yang-resolverd daemon.

workaround: Restart yang-resolverd daemon to reestablish the operation of the daemon. Restart hpe-restd daemon to make a clean connection to YANG after restarting the yang-resolverd daemon.

description: Symptom: Users experience a complete traffic drop for multicast and broadcast flows when the custom prefix is changed to /24, and the default range is also set to /24. Scenario: This issue occurs in a network setup involving multiple switches. When the custom prefix length and default length are both set to /24, multicast traffic fails to encapsulate properly, leading to a total loss of multicast traffic. Workaround: Clear the multicast route using clear ip mroute.

description: Symptom: Clients are unable to onboard on to a LAG interface. Scenario: This issue may occur when all interfaces are enabled on a LAG interface and the LLDP nbr is not learned on all interfaces. This disables the interfaces where LLDP nbr is learned. Workaround: Shut then no shut the port or shut all interfaces on the port and then no shut.

description: Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue occurs after deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-add the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. Workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

description: Symptom: During bulk configuration pushes from Central, ovsdb-server crashes and triggers an automatic switch reboot. Scenario: This issue occurs when a bulk config push is applied on a switch running 17.0001. Workaround: Avoid large multi-edit bulk pushes from Central; apply configuration changes in smaller batches and monitor.

description: Symptom: The MOD feature inspects packets punted to the local LC that are marked for drop. If IPFIX is enabled, these packets undergo further processing to match drop reasons (for example, IP exceptions or hardware drops). If IPFIX is not enabled, the system skips further processing and discards the memory allocated during inspection. Scenario: This issue occurs in a Multi-LC deployment, where there is a corner case where ingress and egress packets traverse different paths. When such a packet is discarded, a small memory leak occurs (approximately the size of hdf packet struct). Over time, these small per-packet leaks accumulate, gradually consuming more memory and potentially impacting the system

description: Symptom: When macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink ports, macsec selftest is stuck in "Initialized" instead of "Passed". Scenario: This issue can occur when macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink using the following steps: 1. On demand macsec selftest execution 2. Empty XCVR bay / 10G XCVR inserted in 25G MACsec uplink ports. Workaround: For on demand MACsec selftest to pass, do not use an empty port bay or 10G XCVRs. Alternatively, when an "Initialized" state is encountered, perform no macsec selftest to recover the port and reperform macsec selftest. Trigger MACsec selftest on boot: configuring macsec selftest and writing to memory followed by a reboot.
symptom: When macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink ports, macsec selftest is stuck in "Initialized" instead of "Passed".
scenario: This issue can occur when macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink using the following steps: 1. On demand macsec selftest execution 2. Empty XCVR bay / 10G XCVR inserted in 25G MACsec uplink ports
workaround: For on demand MACsec selftest to pass, do not use an empty port bay or 10G XCVRs. Alternatively, when an "Initialized" state is encountered, perform no macsec selftest to recover the port and reperform macsec selftest. Trigger MACsec selftest on boot: configuring macsec selftest and writing to memory followed by a reboot.

MACSec (Bug ID 402773): Symptom: When macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink ports, macsec selftest is stuck in "Initialized" instead of "Passed". Scenario: This issue can occur when macsec selftest is executed on 25G MACsec uplink using the following steps: 1. On demand macsec selftest execution 2. Empty XCVR bay / 10G XCVR inserted in 25G MACsec uplink ports. Workaround: For on demand MACsec selftest to pass, do not use an empty port bay or 10G XCVRs. Alternatively, when an "Initialized" state is encountered, perform no macsec selftest to recover the port and reperform macsec selftest. Trigger MACsec selftest on boot: configuring macsec selftest and writing to memory followed by a reboot.
